name: SLB Limited

profile.description: SLB N.V. engages in the provision of technology for the energy industry worldwide. The company operates through four divisions: Digital & Integration, Reservoir Performance, Well Construction, and Production Systems. The company provides field development and hydrocarbon production, carbon management, and integration of adjacent energy systems; reservoir interpretation and data processing services for exploration data; and well construction and production improvement services and products. It also offers subsurface geology and fluids evaluation information; stimulation services to restore or enhance well productivity through hydraulic fracturing, matrix stimulation, and water treatment; and intervention services to oil and gas operators. In addition, the company offers mud logging, directional drilling, measurement-while-drilling, and logging-while-drilling services, as well as engineering support services; supplies drilling fluid systems; designs, manufactures, and markets roller cone and fixed cutter drill bits; bottom-hole-assembly and borehole enlargement technologies; well planning, well drilling, engineering, supervision, logistics, procurement, and contracting of third parties, as well as drilling rig management solutions; and drilling equipment and services, as well as land drilling rigs and related services. Further, it provides artificial lift; supplies packers, safety valves, sand control technology, and various intelligent systems; midstream production systems; valves, chokes, actuators, and surface trees; and OneSubsea, an integrated solutions, products, systems, and services, including wellheads, subsea trees, manifolds and flowline connectors, control systems, connectors, and services. SLB N.V. was formerly known as Schlumberger Limited and change its name to SLB N.V. in October 2025. The company was founded in 1926 and is based in Houston, Texas.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): SLB has transformed from a traditional oilfield services company into a technology and digital solutions provider, with Digital ARR exceeding $1B (+15% YoY) and Data Center Solutions revenue growing 121% YoY [1]. The ChampionX acquisition added production chemistry. AI Deployment (1/2): AI-powered subsurface modeling, drilling automation, and digital workflows deployed at scale across customers. Digital ARR of $1B+ demonstrates commercial AI/digital traction [1]. Market Share (1/2): Largest oilfield services company globally with $8.72B Q1 2026 revenue. Pipeline (1/2): Data Center Solutions growing 121% suggests a meaningful new business vector beyond traditional oilfield. R&D Intensity (1/1): Industry-leading R&D in digital oilfield and subsurface technology.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): SLB's purpose of 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' is more specific and forward-looking than peers. The rebranding from Schlumberger to SLB signals genuine strategic evolution. Community Impact (1/2): Sustainability programs and community investment across global operations. Employee Retention (1/2): Large global workforce with strong engineering culture. Insider Buying (1/2): Management equity stakes signal conviction.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B grew 3% YoY, with production systems up 23% driven by ChampionX [1]. Net income of $752M declined 6% as Middle East disruptions and well construction headwinds offset growth. North America surged 26% to $2.17B. Digital ARR of $1B+ provides recurring revenue visibility. The mix shift toward technology and digital services is gradually improving margin quality.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): SLB's core oilfield operations (subsurface measurement, drilling, production) require physical presence and specialized equipment that AI cannot replace [1]. However, SLB's digital/consulting revenue faces competition from tech companies entering energy data analytics. AI Opportunity (1/1): Digital ARR of $1B+ and Data Center Solutions growing 121% demonstrate meaningful AI/tech revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: SLB has demonstrated infinite game thinking through the deliberate transformation from a traditional oilfield services company to a technology-driven energy solutions provider. The rebranding, digital investments, and Data Center Solutions expansion show willingness to evolve the business model in service of a longer-term vision.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'Driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' is genuine and reflected in strategic investments in digital, new energy, and Data Center Solutions [1]. The cause drives real capital allocation decisions.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Strong engineering culture with global workforce stability.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): SLB competes constructively with HAL and Baker Hughes while expanding into technology spaces.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): Transformation from Schlumberger to SLB, expanding into digital and Data Center Solutions represents meaningful evolution [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): SLB invested in digital transformation and Data Center Solutions before market validation.

investment_thesis: SLB delivered Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B (+3%) as the world's largest oilfield services company, with strategic growth vectors emerging — Digital ARR exceeded $1B (+15%) and Data Center Solutions grew 121% [1]. The ChampionX acquisition drove production systems up 23%. North America surged 26%. The transition from cyclical oilfield services to recurring digital revenue is gradually de-risking the business model.
thesis_continued: Net income declined 6% to $752M in Q1 2026 as Middle East disruptions and well construction weakness offset growth [1]. International revenue fell 4% despite the headline growth. The company remains heavily exposed to oil and gas capex cycles. Data Center Solutions, while growing fast, is a small contributor relative to the $35B+ revenue base. ChampionX integration risk exists.
key_catalysts: Data Center Solutions revenue growing 121% YoY as SLB leverages subsurface expertise for hyperscaler energy and cooling needs
Digital ARR exceeding $1B and growing 15% YoY, gradually shifting the revenue mix toward higher-margin recurring technology services
North America recovery continuing from the 26% Q1 2026 growth driven by ChampionX and improving completions activity
key_risks: Oil price decline from global recession compressing international E&P capex, which drives 74% of SLB revenue
Middle East geopolitical disruption worsening and reducing activity in a key high-margin region
Data Center Solutions growth failing to reach meaningful scale relative to the traditional oilfield business
sources: Yahoo Finance — SLB Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
